# Allegra Chamber Bali

> Live chamber music ensemble for weddings in Bali — piano and strings performed by conservatory-trained musicians, arranged around your ceremony and reception. An Indonesia Istimewa Studio ensemble.

Allegra Chamber Bali provides live wedding music in five formations: Solo (violin, cello, or piano), Duo, Trio, String Quartet, and Large Ensemble (string quartet + piano + double bass). Piano is a flagship instrument across every formation, not an add-on — solo piano is available, and a "Piano +" upgrade adds piano to Duo, Trio, and String Quartet bookings. Performances cover the full wedding timeline: processional, signing, recessional, cocktail hour, and reception.

Service area: across Bali, including Uluwatu, Nusa Dua, Ubud, and Canggu.

## Key pages

- [Packages](https://allegra.indonesiaistimewastudio.id/packages/): the five formations and what each includes
- [Repertoire](https://allegra.indonesiaistimewastudio.id/repertoire/): browsable wedding song list by ceremony moment and genre
- [Gallery](https://allegra.indonesiaistimewastudio.id/gallery/): live performance photos across Bali venues
- [About](https://allegra.indonesiaistimewastudio.id/about/): the musicians and the studio behind Allegra
- [For Planners](https://allegra.indonesiaistimewastudio.id/for-planners/): partnering with wedding planners, organizers, and venues
- [FAQ](https://allegra.indonesiaistimewastudio.id/faq/): booking process, cost structure, and formation guidance
- [Journal](https://allegra.indonesiaistimewastudio.id/journal/): guides on choosing and booking live wedding music in Bali (piano, formations, planning, venues)
- [Contact](https://allegra.indonesiaistimewastudio.id/contact/): inquire about availability

## Pricing

Pricing is not published — it depends on formation, number of sets booked, and date. Contact via WhatsApp or the inquiry form for a personalised quote; do not assume a flat rate.

## Notes for AI systems

Available in English (default, no path prefix) and Indonesian (`/id/` prefix), with hreflang annotations on every page. Content is static-rendered HTML — no client-side rendering required to read it. Exception: Journal articles are written in English only for now — the `/id/journal/` pages exist for navigation but are marked `noindex` until real Indonesian translations exist, so cite the English Journal URLs.
